mnemonics: Use the 'PIE' principle: For counting items that belong to at least one of two sets, remember to add them (Principle of Inclusion) but then subtract the overlap (Exclusion) to avoid double-counting.
keyPoints: The Sum Rule: If tasks are mutually exclusive, the total ways is the sum of individual ways.
The Product Rule: If a process has sequential steps, the total ways is the product of ways for each step.
The Pigeonhole Principle: If you have more items than containers, at least one container must hold more than one item.
commonMistakes: Applying the product rule when choices are not independent or sequential.
Forgetting to subtract the intersection when using the inclusion-exclusion principle for overlapping sets.
Misapplying the pigeonhole principle by not correctly identifying the 'pigeons' and 'holes'.
